claim: Evidence schema, prompt, readiness contract, and tests all distinguish project, external, user, and unknown evidence scopes.

evidence.summary: DEMAND_EVIDENCE_RESULT_SCHEMA_DEFINITION defines evidence schema with explicit scope field and validates it as project | external | user | unknown
evidence.why: This is the canonical evidence schema definition that requires scope declaration.

evidence.summary: VALID_EVIDENCE_SCOPES constant defines the four allowed scope values: project, external, user, unknown
evidence.why: This set is used throughout the codebase to validate scope declarations.

evidence.summary: evidenceRecordScope() function analyzes evidence records and classifies them into project, external, user, or unknown scopes based on path, url, source, and scope fields
evidence.why: This function implements the scope classification logic that distinguishes evidence types.

evidence.summary: evidenceRecordHasDeclaredScope() function checks if an evidence record explicitly declares a valid scope from VALID_EVIDENCE_SCOPES
evidence.why: This validation ensures all evidence records declare their scope.

evidence.summary: evidenceRecordHasProjectLocator() function verifies project-scoped evidence includes a concrete file path that is not a URL
evidence.why: This enforces that project evidence must have a repo-relative path or file locator.

evidence.summary: evidenceScopeDeclarationIssues() function generates blockers for evidence records that fail to declare a valid scope
evidence.why: This is the readiness contract enforcement that requires scope declarations.

evidence.summary: projectEvidenceBlockingIssues() function blocks readiness when project facts are not supported by project-scoped evidence with concrete locators
evidence.why: This is the core readiness contract that prevents external-only evidence from satisfying existing-project claims.

evidence.summary: VALID_EVIDENCE_SCOPES constant mirrors the router definition with the same four scope values
evidence.why: Evidence dispatch system uses the same scope validation.

evidence.summary: evidenceScopeErrors() function validates each evidence record has a valid scope from VALID_EVIDENCE_SCOPES
evidence.why: This validation runs during evidence result normalization.

evidence.summary: buildDemandEvidenceAgentPrompt() generates agent prompts that explicitly require scope declaration on every evidence record with detailed instructions about project vs external evidence
evidence.why: This is the agent prompt that instructs evidence agents to distinguish scopes.

evidence.summary: Agent prompt hard rules state 'Every evidence record must include scope: project, external, user, or unknown' and 'Project facts require project-scoped evidence from code, tests, docs, config, logs, or artifacts, with a repo-relative path or file locator'
evidence.why: These are the explicit instructions to agents about scope requirements.

evidence.summary: Required JSON shape for evidence results includes scope field as 'project | external | user | unknown' and source field with project_code, project_test, project_docs, external_web, user options
evidence.why: This is the output schema that agents must follow.

evidence.summary: Test case 'external-only evidence cannot satisfy existing project facts' verifies PROJECT_FACT_REQUIRES_PROJECT_EVIDENCE blocker is raised when evidence is external-only
evidence.why: This test proves external evidence cannot satisfy existing-project claims.

evidence.summary: Test case 'project evidence requires a concrete project locator' verifies PROJECT_EVIDENCE_PATH_REQUIRED blocker is raised when project scope lacks a path field
evidence.why: This test proves project evidence must have a concrete file locator.

evidence.summary: Test case 'external URLs cannot be relabeled as project evidence' verifies PROJECT_EVIDENCE_PATH_REQUIRED blocker is raised when scope=project but url field is present
evidence.why: This test proves URLs cannot be mislabeled as project evidence.

evidence.summary: Test case 'evidence records must declare valid scope explicitly' verifies EVIDENCE_SCOPE_REQUIRED blocker is raised when scope field is missing
evidence.why: This test proves scope declaration is mandatory.

evidence.summary: Test case 'agent evidence without explicit scope is blocked during normalization' verifies agents that return evidence without scope are normalized to blocked with EVIDENCE_SCOPE_REQUIRED
evidence.why: This test proves the dispatch system enforces scope validation.

evidence.summary: Test case 'non-missing status notes in missing arrays do not block readiness' verifies that status notes like 'No missing data identified' are correctly filtered and don't block
evidence.why: This test proves the missing field sanitization works correctly.

evidence.summary: inspectDemandPrdReadiness() function orchestrates all evidence validation including scope validation, project locator requirements, and evidence agreement checks
evidence.why: This is the main readiness contract that enforces all evidence scope rules.

evidence.summary: buildBoundarySnapshot() and diffBoundarySnapshots() functions implement boundary enforcement by hashing project files before and after agent execution to detect mutations
evidence.why: This proves the boundary system can detect any project file mutations during evidence gathering.

evidence.summary: Test case 'boundary blocks project file mutation even when agent results pass' verifies BOUNDARY_PROJECT_MUTATION blocker is raised when agents modify project files
evidence.why: This test proves the boundary system enforces the no-mutation guarantee.

evidence.summary: Test case 'git-backed boundary detects untracked project mutations' verifies boundary detection works with git repositories and detects new files
evidence.why: This test proves boundary detection works in git repos.

evidence.summary: Test case 'non-git boundary fallback detects modified project files and ignores artifacts' verifies boundary detection falls back to filesystem walking when git is unavailable and ignores artifact directory writes
evidence.why: This test proves boundary detection works in non-git repos and allows artifact writes.

result.notes: All four evidence scopes (project, external, user, unknown) are clearly distinguished in schema, validation logic, agent prompts, readiness contract, and tests. The system correctly blocks external-only evidence for existing-project facts and requires concrete project locators for project-scoped evidence.
